[20-22] A white cloth lies flat on the table with a purple gloved hand visible at the edge. The Neogen logo appears in the corner.

[28-34] The camera zooms in on a person wearing purple gloves holding a metal syringe device in their left hand, suspending the nozzle just above the surface of blue liquid in a left plastic container. Their right hand holds clear tubing submerged in the soapy blue water, with the weighted filter end visible resting at the bottom. A white lab coat and "NEOGEN" logo persist in the background. [34-36] The camera returns to a wide shot as the person transfers the clear intake tubing from the blue liquid in the left tub to the clear water in the right tub, positioning the metal nozzle over the clear liquid for rinsing.

[38-40] A person wearing a white coat and purple gloves holds a metal syringe device over a tub of clear liquid, using their right hand to stabilize the submerged tubing while the left hand grips the handle. [40-48] In a close-up revealing a "Neogen" name tag, the individual holds the blue-handled device horizontally. They use their right hand to grasp and pull the rear plunger assembly backward to adjust the mechanism, while the left hand maintains a firm grip on the instrument's body.

A person in a white coat with a Neogen tag and purple gloves holds a blue syringe device, stabilizing the handle with the left hand while the right index finger points at the rear adjustment knob before grasping the plunger assembly. The right hand actively grips and twists the rear plunger assembly counter-clockwise to unscrew the mechanism from the back of the instrument. Upon completing the disassembly, the person places a small metal cylinder next to a grey ring on a white cloth lying on the table near lubricant bottles and blue packets.

[36-38] A person in a white coat and purple gloves holds a metal syringe device over a right tub filled with clear liquid, with clear tubing submerged and the nozzle positioned just above the surface. [58-60] The person holds a blue syringe device horizontally, gripping the handle while manipulating the rear plunger assembly, with a Neogen logo visible on the coat pocket. [60-64] The blue handle mechanism is held disassembled without the clear barrel, exposing the metal rod. The clear intake tube remains submerged in the blue liquid within the left tub as the person holds the assembly over the two containers, gripping the metal plunger rod and blue handle.

[66-72] A person in a white coat with a 'Neogen' tag and purple gloves holds a blue handle mechanism with a metal plunger rod, keeping its clear intake tube submerged in the blue liquid of the left tub. [72-74] The individual moves the device over the right tub filled with clear liquid before placing the blue mechanism onto a white cloth on the table near metal parts and coiled tubing.

[74-78] A person wearing a white coat and purple gloves sets a blue syringe mechanism down onto a white cloth on the table. The device is positioned near small metal parts and coiled tubing. Two plastic tubs containing liquid sit to the left, while bottles and packets remain visible on the right side of the surface.
[78-82] The person holds up a small clear bottle labeled "PRIMA TECH FOOD GRADE LUBRICANT" in their left hand. The blue syringe mechanism remains held in the right hand. The individual tilts the clear bottle to apply liquid onto the metal tip of the blue syringe mechanism held in their right hand.

[84-90] A person wearing a white coat and purple gloves holds a clear lubricant bottle near the metal tip of a blue syringe device. They apply lubricant to the end of the metal rod while gripping the blue handle with the right hand, dispensing liquid onto the tip. The Neogen logo is visible in the bottom right corner. [90-92] The person places the blue syringe handle on the white cloth and picks up a small clear plastic component with their right hand. The blue device rests nearby next to the lubricant bottle. They hold the clear piece while the left hand holds a small metal part.

[82-84] A person in a white coat and purple gloves dispenses liquid from a clear lubricant bottle onto the metal rod of a blue "PRIMA TECH" syringe device held in the right hand. [92-94] The individual holds a small metal component in the left hand and a clear plastic piece in the right, fitting them together. [94-96] With the assembled part in the left hand, the right hand reaches for a small metal ring near coiled clear tubing on a white table containing two tubs of liquid. [96-98] The person aligns the metal component, now capped with grey, to the front of the blue handle mechanism to attach it.

From 98 to 106 seconds, a person dressed in a white coat and purple gloves connects a metal component with a grey cap to the blue syringe handle. The text "PRIMA TECH" is visible on the blue handle as the parts are joined together. They twist the grey cap to secure the glass chamber to the blue handle. Then, the person pulls the blue trigger lever backward with their left hand. This action extends the metal plunger rod mechanism outward. They continue to pull the blue trigger lever backward with the left hand, extending the metal plunger rod fully outward from the device body, while the right hand holds the handle steady near the attached glass chamber.

[106-114] A person in a white coat and purple gloves holds a blue device. From 106s to 110s, the left hand keeps the trigger lever pulled back, ensuring the metal plunger rod stays fully extended, while the right hand supports the handle body near the attached glass chamber and metal tip. From 110s to 114s, the left hand shifts to grasp the extended metal plunger rod directly, while the right hand continues to stabilize the blue handle. The device remains extended with the glass chamber visible. The logo "NEOGEN CORPORATION" is in the bottom right.

A person in a white coat and purple gloves holds the blue handle mechanism of the syringe with the clear intake tube submerged in blue liquid in the left tub, while the right hand grips the metal plunger rod [64-66]. A right tub contains clear liquid, and the Neogen logo is visible on the coat. The individual maintains their grip on the blue device, with the left hand holding the extended metal plunger rod steady and the right hand supporting the handle body near the glass chamber [114-120]. The device stays fully extended in a static position with the metal rod protruding outward. The Neogen Corporation logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

[122-130] The video displays a static light green background. At the top center, the black "NEOGEN CORPORATION" logo appears with a flask graphic containing a DNA helix on the left and a blue circular "PRIMA TECH" emblem on the right. Centered below is the text "If you have any questions, please contact Neogen". Further down, contact details are listed: "800/477-8201 or 859/254-1221" and the email "primatech@neogen.com". At the bottom, a copyright notice reads "© 2014 Neogen Corporation". The screen remains unchanged throughout the duration.
